pi.name: Professor Brian Sia Jia Xu
pi.title: Principal Investigator
pi.affiliation: School of Electrical & Electronic Engineering, Nanyang Technological University
pi.biography:
Brian is a Singaporean electrical engineer specializing in large-scale photonic integration and high-performance semiconductor lasers. Leveraging these capabilities, his research interests span GPS-free navigation, THz–GHz/MHz optoelectronic systems, gyroscopes, instantaneous chemical and biological sensing, and wireless RF communication based on cutting-edge laser platforms.

He received his B.Eng. from Nanyang Technological University in 2017, having spent a year (2014–2015) at the University of Strathclyde, UK. He went on to earn his Ph.D. from Nanyang Technological University in 2021, where he was elected Valedictorian of his graduating class. During his graduate studies, he received scholarships to work at the Tokyo Institute of Technology, Japan (2018) and Intel Labs, Santa Clara, US (2020).

Upon graduation, Brian joined a prominent Singapore-based silicon photonics startup as Head of Optical and Product Engineering, where he spearheaded commercial platform development. In 2022, he was one of only two annual recipients of the prestigious Ministry of Education–NTU CoE International Postdoctoral Fellowship, which he pursued at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology from 2022 to 2024. In the fall of 2024, Brian joined the School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering at Nanyang Technological University as an Assistant Professor.

Brian was the first to establish a research collaboration with Samsung outside of South Korea and the US. To date, he has secured up to SGD 4.9 million in research funding, with SGD 4.2 million as sole PI. The intellectual property he has authored has attracted industry licensing fees in excess of SGD 900,000. He has served as conference chair at the Photonics@SG 10th anniversary and has delivered numerous invited talks, including at SEMICON 2024 under the Key National Capabilities section and the inaugural Tech Talk at the DSO National Laboratories Quantum Sensing Center (QSC). He serves as a reviewer for Advanced Optical Materials, Journal of Lightwave Technology, ACS Photonics, Optics Express/Letters, and JOSA B. Brian has been the corresponding author in internationally renowned journals such as Nature Communications, and his work has been featured in media outlets including MIT News and Optics.org.
researchInterests: Unification of the THz and the GHz/MHz
